authoritative_sources.title: Customer Specific Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IATF Global Oversight is the official public steward for IATF governance content, and its customer-specific requirements portal is the authoritative public pointer to OEM requirement sets that materially change automotive parts workflow, evidence, and approval boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: prerequisite review, customer-program dependency checks, and escalation when OEM-specific rules are needed

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Automotive Quality Management System Requirements for Sub-Tier Suppliers (MAQMSR)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IATF publishes MAQMSR as an official automotive quality reference for supplier discipline, nonconformance posture, change handling, and customer-facing evidence expectations relevant to parts operations and escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: supplier-quality review, nonconformance handling, and release-boundary escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Automotive Core Tools
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG is a recognized automotive industry body and its core tools page is the authoritative public overview for APQP, Control Plan, FMEA, MSA, SPC, and PPAP materials used to define automotive part readiness, approval, and control vocabulary.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: readiness review, process-control awareness, PPAP boundary handling, and service-parts distinction checks
used_for: service parts versus production parts context

authoritative_sources.title: MMOG/LE
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG's MMOG/LE page is the recognized public reference for automotive materials management and logistics evaluation framing, including inventory, shipping, transportation, receiving, and in-house materials handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: inventory readiness, receiving, line feed, shipment, and in-house material handling review

authoritative_sources.title: Traceability
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized public standards body for global identification and traceability standards and its traceability resources define cross-enterprise event and identifier expectations relevant to automotive parts flows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: part, container, and shipment traceability review across suppliers, plants, warehouses, and downstream customers

authoritative_sources.title: EPCIS Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 EPCIS is the authoritative public event-capture standard used to represent what happened to identified objects across supply-chain systems, making it directly relevant to automotive container, part, and movement traceability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: event capture and reconciliation for receiving, packing, shipping, and handoff events

authoritative_sources.title: Core Business Vocabulary (CBV) Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 CBV is the recognized public reference for business-step and event vocabulary that keeps ERP, WMS, MES, supplier, and logistics traceability evidence semantically aligned.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cross-system event naming, inventory-state reconciliation, and business-step semantics

authoritative_sources.title: Labelling
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Odette is a recognized automotive supply-chain standards body, and its labelling guidance is a public workflow reference for the Global Transport Label, Odette label profiles, and VIN-label handling used by OEM and Tier 1 networks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: packout, container labelling, ASN alignment, and vehicle or service-parts labeling review

authoritative_sources.title: Manufacturer Recalls Portal (MRP) User Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA is the U.S. vehicle safety regulator, and its MRP guidance is the authoritative public reference for Part 573 recall reporting workflow boundaries that parts operations must escalate instead of resolving informally.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: safety-defect, recall, and formal reporting boundary review

workflow_stages.name: order intake and readiness review
workflow_stages.description: Review order type, part number, revision, approved source, label profile, inventory allocation, and required customer-program prerequisites before parts work can begin.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: part picking, sequencing, kitting, and in-process execution
workflow_stages.description: Validate correct part identity, container status, sequence or kanban triggers, and throughput constraints while keeping PPAP, supplier, and engineering boundaries explicit.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: exception handling for holds, damaged material, mislabels, and unsupported substitutions
workflow_stages.description: Track quarantine, quality hold, serial mismatch, damaged container, relabel, rework, and supplier-pressure cases while preserving formal approval ownership.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: traceability and inventory reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that ERP, MES, WMS, ASN, label, and supplier records reconcile across part, revision, serial, lot, container, and location identifiers before progression or shipment is recommended.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence and release-boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Close parts work only when pick, pack, ship, sequence, inventory, and signoff evidence are present, and escalate formal quality, engineering, supplier-quality, or recall decisions instead of absorbing those approvals.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

artifacts: production orders, service-part orders, pull signals, sequence releases, and pack instructions
part masters, engineering levels, supersession chains, approved-source and PPAP status, and change-control records
inventory allocations, license plates, serial or lot history, container IDs, warehouse movements, and line-feed confirmations
supplier ASN records, EDI releases, shipping labels, global transport labels, and packout evidence
quality holds, nonconformance reports, containment records, quarantine status, concession or deviation requests, and release records
shipment confirmations, proof-of-pack, pick confirmations, cycle-count or reconciliation records, and completion signoff artifacts
escalation notes, shortage summaries, supplier-quality requests, and recall-boundary notifications
systems_of_record: supplier management or supplier portal systems

allowed: classify parts work as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, on hold, rework-pending, shipment-ready, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, part records, revision context, CSR context, and systems-of-record evidence needed to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed status updates, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority
escalate: final quality release, concession or deviation approval, or formal nonconformance disposition
engineering change, supersession approval, or use-as-is authority
supplier approval, broker-buy approval, or formal supplier-quality disposition
safety-defect analysis, recall filings, or regulator-facing communication
refuse: start, sequence, ship, or continue work when required revision, label, approved-source, or traceability evidence is missing and would change the outcome
mark parts work complete without pick, pack, ship, inventory, or signoff evidence
bypass traceability, hold, release, label, or quality controls to protect throughput or service levels

domain_failure_modes: the wrong part revision, supersession, or source is used because authoritative engineering and approved-source records were not reconciled
ERP, MES, WMS, ASN, and label systems conflict on serial, lot, container, quantity, or location state for the same parts flow
quarantined or nonconforming material is sequenced, shipped, or consumed because hold and release boundaries were treated as throughput issues
parts work is declared complete without inventory decrement, shipment confirmation, label scans, or required signoff evidence
supplier substitutions, broker buys, or relabel requests are treated as routine scheduling decisions instead of supplier-quality, engineering, or quality escalations
customer-specific requirements or label profiles are assumed rather than retrieved from authoritative program records
the lane drifts into final quality, engineering, supplier approval, sourcing, recall, or safety authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Customer-specific requirement applicability by OEM, program, and region
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: As of April 10, 2026, the IATF customer-specific requirements portal shows multiple OEM-specific sources that can materially narrow parts workflow, labeling, and escalation rules. Tenants still must specify the controlling OEM, customer program, and region before the lane treats a specific CSR set as binding.

AIAG's public core-tools page references a Service PPAP supplement, which means the exact approval and evidence posture can differ for current-production parts versus service parts. Tenant context is required before the lane treats a particular PPAP path or service-parts exception as controlling.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Regional label profile and transport-label variant selection
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Odette and joint automotive labelling references define global and regional label profiles, but plants and OEMs may still mandate specific data content or profile variants in purchasing and logistics agreements. Tenant packaging and customer-routing rules must identify the required profile before relabel or shipment decisions are made.

notes: Authoritative part, revision, inventory, supplier, container, and release records are the ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier, EDI, label, PLM, and logistics records designated by the tenant, constrained by IATF, AIAG, GS1, Odette, and NHTSA public baselines where applicable.

The lane stops before formal quality release, engineering change approval, supplier approval, sourcing decisions, and safety or recall authority even when line rate or service level is under pressure.
